_note: GH #170 — the shell system's deep responsive pass (Kim's 'this still looks like absolute garbage' verdict on the docs-site chrome at narrow). OUTSIDE-IN: the whole shell family crossed — ui-super-shell's frame anatomy (header bar [side-toggle | bar-content | side-toggle] · narrow-tabs strip · middle [rail | pane* | pane-resizer? | canvas | pane* | rail] · footer bar), the band model (today ONE 40rem line, shell-breakpoint.ts), the per-side narrow arms (collapse | stack | tabs), the thin presets (ui-workspace-shell / ui-chat-shell, both parameterizations of the grammar), the independent M1 primitive ui-app-shell (5-region grid, per-region hide/stack/toggle), and the real consumers (site/pages/_page.ts's chrome on raw ui-super-shell with narrow-start='stack' + ui-nav-rail collapse='menu'; agent-admin on ui-chat-shell narrow-end='tabs'; the demo pages). INSIDE-OUT: the fourteen actions a1–a14 below. THE CROSS FOUND SIX UNHOSTED ACTIONS in the shipped structure: (1) no compact band — one shared 40rem container line collapses the INNER nested shell BEFORE the outer app ring (inner container is always narrower), the OPPOSITE of Kim's GH #44 outer-in cascade, and leaves the docs nav pane consuming ~40% of a 640–840px viewport that ADR-0150 already rules 'compact reading width'; (2) #makeToggle renders BOTH side toggles unconditionally — a dead end-toggle ships on the docs site (no end side authored), and at narrow its data-narrow-open overlay arm stacks CONFLICTING CSS onto a narrow-start='stack' side (position:absolute over the in-flow stacked nav — the 'overlay pushes/overlaps' symptom in Kim's screenshots); (3) the toggle glyph is statically 'list' and aria-expanded tracks only the WIDE collapsed-* props — at narrow the overlay state (data-narrow-open) drives neither, so both the glyph and the ARIA state LIE below 40rem, and a stale data-narrow-open survives a resize back to wide; (4) the JS band read is a raw `getBoundingClientRect().width < 640` px literal in super-shell.ts — bypassing shell-breakpoint.ts's named 40REM constant (px≠rem under a non-16px root font, the exact drift the LLD-C7/GH #99 gate exists to prevent, unreachable by that gate because it only sweeps CSS literals); (5) the narrow overlay has NO dismissal beyond re-tapping the toggle — no scrim, no Escape, no focus contract; (6) super-shell.css has ZERO scrollbar-width rules on its four owned scroll regions (pane overflow-y:auto · active segment overflow-y:auto · narrow-tabs overflow-x:auto · the overlay-restored side) — the only shell-family surface NOT carrying the fleet's hidden-scroller seam (command-modal/menu #166/card/conversation/surface-host/agent-admin all do), and the scrollFade trait it needs is not on @agent-ui/components' public barrel (paneResize IS — the export precedent). Verified no-ops: ui-app-shell owns NO scroll region (its regions' scroll is consumer content — the seam is vacuous there) and its app-surfaces-m4.lld.md LLD-C11 collapse='toggle' Show/Hide disclosure is a DIFFERENT affordance altitude than the header side-toggles (named non-adoption, not a gap). Sequencing fact: this branch (stacked on build-52-shell-grammar-rehost / PR #175) does NOT contain main's GH #166 menu fix (merge 181dad9) — no file overlap, but the build phase must treat main's menu.css as the convention reference and land after PR #175. Design record: ADR-0155 (proposed) + shell-archetypes-m5.spec.md v0.4 §9 (SPEC-R8/R9/R10, AC13–AC18) + lld/shell-responsive.lld.md.

nodes.label: shell responsive system (GH #170) — band ladder + toggle affordance law + scrollbar seam + the docs-site narrow story, designed once at the grammar ceiling (ADR-0151 discipline)
SHELL_COMPACT_BREAKPOINT_REM = 52.5 joins shell-breakpoint.ts beside the existing 40 — two named lines, the consistency gate extended to sweep both
nodes.accept: `npx vitest run packages/agent-ui/app/src/shell-breakpoint.test.ts` — the test enumerates BOTH constants and reds if any shell-family CSS literal (40rem sites; the new 52.5rem compact arms) drifts from its named source; the constant's doc comment cites ADR-0150's 52.5rem/840px M3 compact-window boundary as the shared number and ADR-0155 as the shell-side decision

nodes.label: the `collapse-band` prop ('narrow' default | 'compact') + the compact CSS arms — collapse-mode sides hide below the shell's band line (query-only, no attribute writes) and toggle-restore as overlays; stack/tabs sides keep their own 40rem reflow line untouched
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` app shard — a collapse-band='compact' shell at 48rem container width computes its collapse-mode side display:none while a sibling default shell at the same width still shows it; the persisted collapsed-* attributes are NOT rewritten by any band crossing (the R4 no-clobber law asserted across a wide→compact→wide round-trip); a depth-2 nesting (compact outer, default inner) collapses the OUTER sides at 52.5rem and the inner panes only at 40rem — the GH #44 outer-in cascade, probed cross-engine

nodes.label: toggle presence law — a side toggle composes ONLY when its side has authored content, and per-mode at narrow: collapse ⇒ toggle stays (the overlay affordance), stack/tabs ⇒ the toggle hides below the line (their narrow anatomy is owned elsewhere)
nodes.accept: `npm test` + browser shard — a shell with no end-side children renders NO [data-part='side-toggle'][data-side='end'] (the docs site's dead-button today, gone); at <40rem a narrow-start='stack' shell computes its start toggle display:none (the conflicting overlay-over-stacked-nav arm is unreachable) and a narrow-*='tabs' side's toggle likewise hides (no dead no-op button)

nodes.label: menu⇄X — the toggle composes BOTH glyphs (list + x, both already in icons.gen.ts) and CSS swaps visibility off [data-narrow-open] INSIDE the band container query, so the X can never leak to wide even with a stale attribute (band-correct by construction, zero JS paint dependency)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` app shard, both engines — at narrow with the side overlay open the toggle's x icon computes visible and the list icon display:none; same attributes at wide computes the inverse; a visual-shard screenshot pins the open-overlay header showing the X

nodes.label: band hygiene + ARIA truth — ONE visibility-only ResizeObserver (attributes only, SPEC-R7c-compliant: never a reparent) clears data-narrow-open on leaving the overlay band and keeps aria-expanded truthful per band (narrow: open===side · wide: !collapsed); every JS band read derives from shell-breakpoint.ts's constants × the live root font-size, retiring super-shell.ts's raw `< 640` px literal
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` app shard — open the overlay at narrow, resize wide: data-narrow-open is cleared and aria-expanded returns to the persisted-collapse truth (no stale X, no lying ARIA); a trip-wire asserts authored-content isConnected identity across the full band round-trip (the survival law, extended to this observer); grep gate: no bare 640/40rem-equivalent px literal remains in super-shell.ts

nodes.label: overlay dismissal contract — a composed-once [data-part='scrim'] (shown at narrow while a side is overlay-open; tap closes), Escape closes, focus moves to the opened pane container (tabindex=-1) and returns to the toggle on close; overlay inline-size caps at min(pane-size, 100cqi − bar-size) so a canvas edge stays visible
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` app shard, both engines — scrim tap and Escape each clear data-narrow-open with focus restored to the toggle; the overlay's rect never spans the full container inline-size at 320px; the scrim never intercepts events at wide (display:none outside the query)

nodes.label: the hidden-scrollbar seam on the shell's owned scroll regions — --ui-super-shell-scrollbar-width: none (consumer-overridable, the command-modal.css/menu #166 shape) applied to pane boxes, active segments, and the narrow-tabs strip; the overlay-restored side inherits the pane rule
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` app shard — an overflowing pane computes scrollbar-width:none with scrollHeight > clientHeight and live scrollTop movement (the menu-overflow.browser.test.ts probe shape), on pane + active segment + narrow-tabs strip, both engines; repointing the token on a consumer restores a visible scrollbar (the override leg)

nodes.label: scroll-fade affordance — scrollFade(host, { viewport }) wired per scrollable pane box / segment (the menu.ts precedent: the fade IS the scroll affordance once the bar hides); requires exporting scrollFade from @agent-ui/components' root barrel (the paneResize/ADR-0023 public-API-widening precedent, named not silent)
nodes.accept: `npm run check` green with app importing { scrollFade } from '@agent-ui/components' (the barrel export exists and tree-shake.test.ts stays green); browser probe: an overflowing pane shows the fade edge treatment at the scrolled-away edge (the scroll-fade trait's own tested contract, exercised through the shell)

nodes.label: preset propagation — ui-workspace-shell's default flips stack→collapse + collapse-band='compact' (it mirrors the docs-site shape by charter, and the site's story changes with ADR-0155); ui-chat-shell adds 'collapse-band' to FORWARD_ATTRS, own defaults UNCHANGED (agent-admin's shipped 640px narrow-tabs parity)
nodes.accept: `npm test` — workspace-shell's composed inner shell carries narrow-start='collapse' + collapse-band='compact'; chat-shell forwards collapse-band verbatim and, unset, composes byte-identically to today (negative control: agent-admin's pinned narrow behavior unmoved)

nodes.label: the docs-site migration (the consumer whose narrow rendering triggered #170) — _page.ts: collapse-band='compact' + narrow-start='collapse' (retiring the stack arrangement); buildNav drops collapse='menu'/collapse-container='ancestor' (the rail renders its full vertical anatomy inside the pane/overlay — TKT-0035's ancestor-container trick retires); _page.css's 40rem stacked-nav media arms retire; the header's brand/Search/Theme row proves out at 360px (no overflow, the Search/Theme affordances stay per the standing header-activation convention)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` site shard — at <52.5rem the nav pane is hidden and the header shows the single menu toggle; tapping opens the nav overlay (full vertical rail, X in the header), scrim/Escape dismiss, link click navigates; at ≥52.5rem the nav renders in-flow with the persisted collapse choice intact (localStorage round-trip pin kept); no horizontal overflow at 360/640/840/1200px, both engines

nodes.label: ui-app-shell disposition — NAMED no-ops with reasons, not silent omissions: it owns zero scroll regions (verified: app-shell.css declares no overflow scroller — region scroll is consumer content), so the scrollbar seam is vacuous there; its app-surfaces-m4.lld.md LLD-C11 collapse='toggle' Show/Hide disclosure is a region-local affordance, NOT a header side-toggle — menu⇄X is deliberately not adopted (different altitude), recorded in app-shell.md
nodes.accept: app-shell.md carries the two named dispositions (seam vacuous · menu⇄X non-adoption with the altitude reasoning); `git diff --stat` for the build shows zero hunks in app-shell.{ts,css} (the negative control)

nodes.label: cross-engine proof at every band — the whole-shape law: composed-shell probes at 360/640/840/1200px container widths (narrow / boundary / compact / wide), Chromium + WebKit, plus visual-shard screenshots of the docs chrome at narrow closed AND open
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — all six shards green with the new probes; the visual shard carries before/after-band screenshots of the site chrome (the artifact Kim's sign-off reviews); the survival trip-wire (n6) and no-horizontal-overflow sweep run inside the same suite

nodes.label: Kim's visual sign-off at narrow — the subjective bar that triggered #170; OUT OF AGENT SCOPE by the issue's own acceptance clause 3 (no agent substitutes for Kim's visual judgment): the handoff names it as the remaining open gate, with the n13 screenshots as the review artifact
nodes.accept: the build-phase handoff and GH #170's Findings comment name Kim's narrow-viewport visual review as the open acceptance gate, pointing at the visual-shard screenshots; the gate is Kim's utterance, never an agent claim

actions.label: show app-sidebars at higher breakpoints, honoring the consumer's persisted collapse choice
hide sidebars below the band line and present a menu toggle in the header
restore a hidden side at narrow as a one-at-a-time overlay, never squeezing the canvas below its floor
swap the toggle glyph menu⇄X while its side's overlay is open, with aria-expanded truthful at every band and no stale X after a resize
dismiss the open overlay by toggle re-tap, scrim tap, or Escape, with a sane focus contract
never render a dead or conflicting toggle (absent side; stack/tabs sides below the line)
hide native scrollbars on every shell-owned scroll region while keeping scroll live, with the scroll-fade edge affordance as the replacement signal
keep JS band reads and CSS band queries from drifting — one named source of truth per line
preserve the no-clobber persistence law across every band crossing
preserve the visibility-only survival law (SPEC-R7c) through every new state change — a live embedded surface is never cycled
collapse outer-in at depth 2 — the app ring's sides before the canvas panes (Kim's GH #44 cascade)
compose the docs-site chrome on all of it so the narrow rendering stops reading broken
propagate to the presets with zero duplication and name ui-app-shell's disposition explicitly
prove the composed result cross-engine at both bands, ending at Kim's own visual sign-off
